file_analyses.file_purpose: Locale and country configuration mapping file that defines country-specific settings for test execution. Contains comprehensive data for each supported country including country name, ISO country code, supported languages, regional grouping (NA/EU/APJ), and payment gateway configuration (PGS or 2CO). Used by test flows to determine locale-specific behavior, language selection, payment gateway routing, and regional test execution.
file_analyses.usage_example: locale_data = load_json_file('test_flows_common/locale_map.json'); country_info = locale_data['United States']; payment_gateway = country_info['card_payment_gateway']
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a critical configuration file for multi-locale test execution. It maps 38 countries across 3 regions (North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific-Japan) with their ISO codes, supported languages, and payment gateway assignments. The payment gateway field determines whether tests should use PGS (Payment Gateway Service) or 2CO (2Checkout) for credit card processing. Countries like Belgium, Canada, Switzerland, and Hong Kong support multiple languages. This file is referenced by test setup functions to configure locale-specific test behavior including language selection, address formatting, payment method availability, and regional API endpoints.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Offer configuration data file containing prepaid and free offer definitions for different countries. Each country has one or more offers with unique GUID, identifier code, type (prepaid/free), amount in cents, and additional payment requirement flag. Used by enrollment and payment test flows to select appropriate offers based on country and test requirements.
file_analyses.usage_example: offers_data = load_json_file('test_flows_common/offers.json'); us_offers = offers_data['US']; free_offer = [o for o in us_offers if o['type'] == 'free'][0]; offer_guid = free_offer['guid']
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This file contains offer definitions used for enrollment testing across different countries. The US has the most diverse offer set including a free trial offer (1 month) and multiple prepaid offers ranging from $3.00 to $60.00. Most European countries share a common prepaid offer (GUID 02ba58f3-8f8a-48d5-8d99-16559ee18ed1, identifier 10232, 6000 cents = $60.00). Germany has an additional low-value offer (500 cents = $5.00) that requires additional payment. The additionalPaymentRequired flag indicates whether a payment method must be provided even for prepaid offers. This data is used by the get_offer() and get_offer_code() functions in test_flows_common.py to generate offer codes for enrollment flows.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Payment method test data file containing credit card numbers, CVV codes, expiration dates, bank account details, and third-party payment credentials for automated payment testing. Includes test data for multiple credit card types (Mastercard, Visa, Amex, Discover), direct debit (IBAN), PayPal accounts, Google Pay, and prepaid-only scenarios. Used by payment flow tests to fill payment forms with valid test credentials.
file_analyses.usage_example: payment_data = get_payment_method_data('credit_card_visa'); card_number = payment_data['credit_card_number']; cvv = payment_data['cvv']; exp_month = payment_data['expiration_month']; exp_year = payment_data['expiration_year']
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This file provides test payment credentials for all supported payment methods in the Instant Ink enrollment flow. The expiration_year field uses a special notation '+6' which means 6 years from the current year - this is dynamically calculated by the get_payment_method_data() function in test_flows_common.py to ensure cards never expire during testing. Credit card numbers are standard test card numbers from payment gateway providers (Stripe, Braintree, etc.). The 3ds_auth_result field indicates expected 3D Secure authentication outcome. Direct debit uses German IBAN test numbers. PayPal and Google Pay use real test accounts with known credentials. The prepaid_only entry is an empty object used when no payment method is required. This data is critical for payment flow automation and is referenced by payment page objects to fill payment forms.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Printer profile configuration file defining printer models (derivative models) with their SKUs organized by region (NA, EU, APJ). Each SKU entry includes the SKU code, easy enrollment capability flag, and supply variant type (IIC or IPH). Used by virtual printer creation and enrollment test flows to select appropriate printer SKUs based on region and enrollment requirements.

supply_variant_types.IIC: Instant Ink Cartridge - 4 separate cartridges (K, C, M, Y)

json_structure.easy_enroll_flag: Indicates whether the printer supports easy enrollment flow without manual claim code entry

file_analyses.usage_example: printer_profiles = load_json_file('test_flows_common/printer_profiles.json'); manhattan_profile = printer_profiles['manhattan_yeti']; na_skus = manhattan_profile['skus']['NA']; easy_enroll_sku = [s for s in na_skus if s['easy_enroll']][0]; sku_code = easy_enroll_sku['sku']; supply_variant = easy_enroll_sku['supplyvariant']
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This file defines 13 printer models with their regional SKU variations. Each printer model has different SKUs for North America (NA), Europe (EU), and Asia-Pacific-Japan (APJ) regions. The supply variant field indicates the cartridge configuration: IIC (Instant Ink Cartridge) uses 4 separate color cartridges (Black, Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) while IPH (Instant Ink Photo) uses 2 cartridges (combined CMY and separate Black). The easy_enroll flag determines whether the printer supports streamlined enrollment without requiring manual claim code entry. Some models like 'novelli' have duplicate SKUs with different easy_enroll values for testing both enrollment paths. This data is used by create_virtual_printer() function to create printers with correct model numbers and supply configurations. The printer_colors() function in test_flows_common.py uses the supply variant to determine expected cartridge colors for supply level validation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive test flow helper module providing common utilities, API integrations, and reusable functions for HP Instant Ink test automation. Contains environment configuration for multiple stacks (pie, stage, production), authentication helpers (HPID, OAuth, token management), virtual printer creation and management, email verification code retrieval, payment processing utilities, subscription state validation, and UI interaction helpers. This is the central utility module imported by most test scripts to access shared functionality.

imports.import_statement: from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_fixed

classes.class_purpose: Named tuple class for storing virtual printer data including entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint. Extends tuple to provide both indexed access and named attribute access to printer information returned by create_virtual_printer() function. Used to pass printer details between printer creation, registration, and claiming functions.
classes.how_to_instantiate: printer = PrinterData(entity_id='ABC123', model_number='1G5L3A', device_uuid='uuid-string', cloud_id='cloud-123', postcard='postcard-data', fingerprint='fingerprint-data')

functions.method_signature: def __new__(cls, entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint)
functions.what_it_does: Creates a new PrinterData tuple instance by calling the parent tuple class __new__ method with all six printer data fields as a tuple. This allows PrinterData to be used as an immutable tuple while also providing named attribute access through the __init__ method.

functions.method_signature: def __init__(self, entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint)
functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrinterData instance by setting named attributes for all six printer data fields. Assigns self.entity_id, self.model_number, self.device_uuid, self.cloud_id, self.postcard, and self.fingerprint from the provided parameters. This allows accessing printer data using both tuple indexing (printer[0]) and named attributes (printer.entity_id).
functions.usage_example: printer = PrinterData('ABC123', '1G5L3A', 'uuid-123', 'cloud-456', 'postcard-data', 'fingerprint-data'); entity = printer.entity_id; model = printer[1]

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Dictionary containing all configuration parameters including stack, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, tenant_country, tenant_country_short, tenant_country_language_code, tenant_address, headless, target, simulator_platform, target_width, target_height, supplyvariant, card_payment_gateway, payment_method

functions.what_it_does: Sets all global module-level configuration variables from the provided args dictionary. Extracts stack, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, tenant country information, display settings, and payment configuration. Then loads the corresponding stack configuration from STACK_CONFIG dictionary to set all environment-specific URLs (instantink_url, agena_url, cartridgepacker_url, fulfillmentservice_url, oss_url, hpid_url, stratus endpoints, ucde endpoints) and client secrets. Also initializes MongoDB and S3 storage configuration. Logs all loaded configuration values for debugging. This function must be called before any test execution to configure the framework for the target environment.

Initializes the test framework by calling set_globals() with configuration values extracted from GlobalState. Reads stack, printer_profile, derivative_model, biz_model, supplyvariant, country, country_code, language_code, address_payload, headless, target, simulator_platform, card_payment_gateway, and payment_method from GlobalState and passes them to set_globals(). Then creates a 'temp' directory for temporary file storage during test execution. This is typically called in conftest.py or test setup fixtures to initialize the framework before test execution.

Generates a unique email address for test tenant creation by appending a random 8-character alphanumeric suffix to 'hello.instantink+' and '@gmail.com'. Uses random.choices() to generate the suffix from lowercase letters and digits. Stores the generated email in the global _tenant_email variable and returns it. The plus-addressing format (hello.instantink+suffix@gmail.com) allows all emails to be delivered to the same Gmail inbox while maintaining unique addresses for each test execution.

Authenticates with Gmail API using OAuth2 refresh token stored in environment variables. Creates a Credentials object with GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN, GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, and GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET. Calls creds.refresh() to obtain a new access token using the refresh token. If refresh fails with invalid_grant or invalid_request error, raises RuntimeError indicating the refresh token needs to be updated. Returns a Gmail API service object built using the refreshed credentials. This service object is used by fetch_verification_code() to read verification emails.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML content of the verification email

functions.what_it_does: Extracts the 6-digit verification code from HPID verification email HTML content. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse the HTML and finds a paragraph element with class 'code'. Extracts the text content from this element using get_text(strip=True) and returns it. If the code element is not found, raises RuntimeError. This is the original extraction method that looks for a specific HTML structure. The newer new_extract_verification_code() function provides more robust extraction with regex fallbacks.

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address to search for verification code

functions.what_it_does: Fetches the HPID verification code from Gmail for the specified tenant email address. Authenticates with Gmail API using authenticate_gmail(). Searches for messages sent to the tenant email within the last 5 minutes using query 'to:{email} newer_than:5m'. Retrieves the most recent message in full format. Extracts the HTML body from the message payload (either from main body or from parts array). Decodes the base64-encoded HTML content. Calls new_extract_verification_code() to extract the 6-digit code from the HTML. Returns the verification code string. The @retry decorator automatically retries up to 3 times with 5-second delays if the function fails (e.g., if no messages are found yet).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute after submitting signup form

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to use for signup. If None, uses global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new HPID account through the HP ID signup page. Navigates to the HPID URL (_hpid_url). Sets viewport to 1366x768. Waits for and clicks the sign-up button. Fills the signup form with DEFAULT_FIRSTNAME, DEFAULT_LASTNAME, provided email (or _tenant_email), and DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks the sign-up submit button. Waits for page load. Executes the optional callback function if provided. Then enters a 60-second polling loop checking if either the OTP code entry screen (#submit-code) or the myaccount page has loaded. If OTP screen appears, calls confirm_code_by_onboarding_center(). If myaccount page loads, calls confirm_code_by_my_account(). Returns the page object. This function handles HPID creation for older enrollment flows.

parameters.name: check_func
parameters.description: Lambda or function to execute for checking a condition

functions.what_it_does: Safely executes a check function and returns its result, catching any exceptions and returning False if an exception occurs. This is used in polling loops to check for element presence or page conditions without crashing if the element doesn't exist yet. Wraps the check_func call in a try-except block that returns False on any exception.

Confirms the HPID verification code through the My Account security page. Clicks the security tab (li[id='security']). Clicks the Verify button. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the 6-digit code from email. Fills the code into the textbox with name 'Enter 6-digit numeric code'. Clicks the Verify button to submit the code. This flow is used when HPID signup redirects to the My Account page for email verification.

Confirms the HPID verification code through the Onboarding Center OTP screen. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the 6-digit code from email. Fills the code into the #code input field. Clicks the #submit-code button. Then attempts to handle the country residence selector if it appears (waits up to 20 seconds for #countryResidence). If the selector appears, clicks it, selects the country using the _tenant_country_short code from a data-value attribute, and clicks the continue button. If the country selector doesn't appear within 20 seconds, logs a debug message and continues. Logs success message when HPID is created. This flow is used when HPID signup shows the OTP entry screen directly.

Retrieves the HPID verification code from email with retry logic. Attempts to call fetch_verification_code(_tenant_email) up to 3 times. If fetch_verification_code() raises an exception (typically because no email has arrived yet), logs a warning and waits 15 seconds before retrying. If all 3 attempts fail, raises RuntimeError. If successful, logs the retrieved code and returns it. This function provides a higher-level retry wrapper around fetch_verification_code() with longer delays between attempts.
functions.usage_example: code = get_verification_code(); # Returns '123456' after retrying if needed

parameters.name: test_requirements
parameters.description: Dictionary containing test requirements including optional plan_pages key

functions.docstring: Determine plan pages from requirements or use default.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan page count to use for enrollment testing. Checks if test_requirements dictionary contains a 'plan_pages' key. If present, returns that value and logs it. If not present, returns the default value '100' and logs that the default is being used. This allows tests to specify custom plan page counts or fall back to a standard default.

parameters.name: plan_type_param
parameters.description: Plan type parameter passed to the function

parameters.name: test_requirements
parameters.description: Dictionary containing test requirements including optional paper_option key

functions.docstring: Determine plan type from parameter or requirements.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan type (ink_only or ink_and_paper) for enrollment. If plan_type_param is not 'ink_only', returns plan_type_param directly and logs it. If plan_type_param is 'ink_only', checks the paper_option key in test_requirements. If paper_option is 'add', returns 'ink_and_paper'. Otherwise returns 'ink_only'. Logs the determined plan type with the paper_option value. This allows tests to specify plan type directly or derive it from paper option requirements.

Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account through the enrollment flow. First tests browser connectivity by navigating to about:blank. Then navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait and 120-second timeout. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Attempts to accept privacy banner if present. Locates and clicks the sign-up button (tries multiple test IDs). Clicks the create account button. Waits for the sign-in form to appear. Waits explicitly for firstName field to be visible and interactive. Fills firstName, lastName, email (_tenant_email), and password (DEFAULT_PASSWORD). Clicks the market checkbox. Clicks sign-up submit. Waits for OTP code entry screen. Retrieves verification code with retry logic (up to 3 attempts with 5-second delays). Fills the code and submits. Waits for the stepper element to appear indicating successful account creation. Navigates to portalshell URL and waits 60 seconds. Attempts to click side menu toggle. Clicks the HP Instant Ink menu item. Returns the page object. This is the primary account creation flow for v2 enrollment tests.

parameters.name: oobe
parameters.description: Boolean flag indicating if this is an OOBE (out-of-box-experience) login flow

functions.what_it_does: Logs into an existing Instant Ink v2 account. Checks if sign-up button is visible (timeout 5 seconds). If not visible, navigates to _instantink_url. Attempts to accept privacy banner if present. Clicks the sign-up button. Waits 5 seconds. Clicks the sign-in button. Fills username field. Clicks 'Use password' link. Fills password field with DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks sign-in button. If oobe parameter is True, waits for device selection radio button to appear (up to 180 seconds), clicks the 'Add a different printer' radio button, waits for continue button, and clicks it. Returns the page object. This function is used to login to existing accounts for testing scenarios that require pre-existing users.

Logs out of the Instant Ink v2 account. Locates the account button using data-testid 'account-button'. Expects it to be visible within 3 seconds. Clicks the account button to open the account menu. Locates the sign-out button using data-testid 'sign-out-button'. Expects it to be visible within 3 seconds. Clicks the sign-out button. Waits for the sign-up button to appear (up to 120 seconds) to confirm logout was successful. This function is used in test teardown or when testing multiple user scenarios.

Onboards an HPID user to the UCDE (Unified Cloud Device Ecosystem) system via OAuth flow. Generates a random GUID for the OAuth state parameter. Constructs the UCDE authorization URL with openid, email, and profile scopes, using _ucde_client_id and REDIRECT_URI. Navigates the page to the authorization URL. Waits for page load. Waits 5 seconds for redirect. Extracts the authorization code from the final redirect URI. Exchanges the authorization code for access_token and id_token by POSTing to the UCDE token endpoint with Basic authentication (client_id:client_secret base64 encoded). Validates that both tokens were received. Creates a UCDE account by POSTing to the accounts endpoint with the id_token, country set, and language. Logs the response. Returns the page object. This function is critical for enabling UCDE-based device management and enrollment flows.

Obtains an OAuth access token for the Gemini client using client credentials grant. Constructs the token URL from _stratus_authz. Creates Basic authentication header by base64 encoding gemini_client_id:gemini_client_secret. POSTs to the token endpoint with grant_type=client_credentials. Raises exception if request fails. Extracts and returns the access_token from the JSON response. This token is used for Gemini API calls that require service-level authentication.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the user. If None, uses global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Obtains an organization-aware user access token for the specified email address through a multi-step token exchange process. Step 1: Gets Gemini client token using get_token_gemini_client(). Step 2: Uses Gemini token to query usermgtsvc for user details by email, extracts userResourceId and idpId. Step 3: Uses Gemini token to query usertenantdetails by userResourceId, extracts tenantResourceId. Step 4: Gets Stratus client token using client credentials grant with stratus_client_id and stratus_client_secret. Step 5: Performs token exchange by POSTing to token endpoint with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange, subject_token=stratus_client_token, requested_user=userResourceId, and tenant_id=tenantResourceId. Extracts the final org-aware access token from the response. Returns a tuple of (org_token, tenant_id). This token has user context and tenant context, allowing API calls to act on behalf of the user within their tenant. Used for device claiming, address management, and subscription operations.
functions.usage_example: org_token, tenant_id = get_org_aware_token('test@example.com'); # Use org_token for API calls

functions.method_signature: def retry_operation(operation, operation_name="Operation", max_attempts=5, delay=2, on_retry=None)

parameters.name: operation_name
parameters.description: Name of the operation for logging

parameters.name: on_retry
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute before each retry

functions.what_it_does: Generic retry wrapper that executes an operation function with automatic retry logic. Attempts to call the operation function up to max_attempts times. Logs each attempt number. If the operation succeeds, logs success and returns the result. If the operation raises an exception, logs the error. If this was the last attempt, logs failure and re-raises the exception. If there are attempts remaining, optionally calls the on_retry callback function (catching and logging any errors from the callback). Waits for the specified delay before the next attempt. This utility is used throughout the module to add retry logic to flaky operations like API calls and printer creation.
functions.usage_example: result = retry_operation(lambda: api_call(), operation_name='API Call', max_attempts=3, delay=5)

functions.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer using the G2 simulator API through a multi-step process with retry logic. Defines a helper function make_request() for HTTP requests. Step 1: POSTs to the simulator printers endpoint with stack, profile, biz_model, offer, fipsflag, and derivative_model to create the printer. Uses retry_operation() with 5 attempts. Extracts entity_id (serial number), model_number, and device_uuid from response. Step 2: POSTs to the register endpoint for the created printer to register it with the cloud. Uses retry_operation() with 5 attempts. Extracts cloud_id from response. Step 3: POSTs to the claimpostcard endpoint to get the claim postcard data. Uses retry_operation() with 3 attempts. Base64 encodes the postcard if it's JSON, otherwise uses it as-is. Step 4: GETs the devicefingerprint endpoint to get the device fingerprint. Uses retry_operation() with 3 attempts. Base64 encodes the fingerprint if it's JSON, otherwise uses it as-is. Creates a PrinterData tuple with all six values. Appends it to the global _printer_created list. Returns the PrinterData object. This function is critical for creating test printers for enrollment and device management testing.
functions.usage_example: printer = create_virtual_printer(); entity_id = printer.entity_id; cloud_id = printer.cloud_id

functions.method_signature: def claim_virtual_printer(org_token, tenant_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, fingerprint)
functions.what_it_does: Claims a virtual printer to a tenant account using the UCDE API with retry logic. Constructs the autoClaimRequest payload with productNumber, uuid, claimPostcard, fingerprint, and selectedBizModel. Attempts to POST to the UCDE devices endpoint under the tenant account up to 10 times. Uses the org_token for Bearer authentication. If the request succeeds (status 200), logs success and returns. If the request fails, logs the error. If this was the last attempt, logs failure and re-raises the exception. Otherwise waits 2 seconds and retries. This function associates the virtual printer with the tenant account, making it available for enrollment.

Adds a shipping address to the tenant account using the Gemini comfe API. Checks if _tenant_address is a list and extracts the first element if so, otherwise uses it directly. Filters out any keys starting with 'fullState' from the address data. POSTs the address payload to the comfe shipping addresses endpoint with tenantId query parameter. Uses org_token for Bearer authentication. Checks if response status is 201 (Created). If not 201, raises RuntimeError. Logs success message. This function is used to pre-populate shipping addresses for enrollment testing.

Creates and claims a virtual printer in one operation. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org_token and tenant_id. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer and get all printer data. Unpacks the PrinterData tuple into entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint. Logs all printer details. Calls claim_virtual_printer() with the org_token, tenant_id, and printer data to claim the printer to the tenant. Logs success. Waits 600 seconds (10 minutes) as a temporary fix for printer availability issues (references bug tickets III-2131 and IIGAR-2774). Returns the PrinterData object. This is a convenience function that combines printer creation and claiming for test setup.

Creates and claims a virtual printer and adds a shipping address in one operation. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org_token and tenant_id. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the PrinterData tuple. Logs printer details. Calls claim_virtual_printer() to claim the printer. Calls add_address_to_tenant() to add the shipping address using the comfe API. Logs that address was added successfully. Logs that it's waiting 10 minutes for printer availability. Waits 600 seconds as a temporary fix for printer availability issues. Returns the PrinterData object. This is a convenience function that combines printer creation, claiming, and address setup for enrollment tests that require all three.

parameters.name: payment_type
parameters.description: Payment method type key. If None, uses global _payment_method or defaults to 'credit_card_master'

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves payment method test data from payment_method_data.json file. If payment_type is None, uses the global _payment_method variable or defaults to 'credit_card_master'. Loads the JSON file using load_json_file(). Validates that the payment_type exists in the data, raising ValueError with available types if not found. Creates a copy of the payment data for the specified type. Checks if the data contains an 'expiration_year' field. If the expiration_year value is a string starting with '+', calculates the actual year by adding the number after '+' to the current year. For example, '+6' becomes current_year + 6. Returns the payment data dictionary with the calculated expiration year. This function is used by payment page objects to get test payment credentials.

Creates an OOBE (Out-Of-Box Experience) registration record in Agena for the specified printer. Constructs a payload with model_number, serial_number (entity_id), country (_tenant_country_short), and empty strings for sku, ink_declaration, supply_types, supply_levels, supply_states, unenrolled_pages, ui_phase, enrollment_kit, jump_id, and ip_address. POSTs to the Agena oobe_registrations endpoint with Bearer authentication using org_token and Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=2'. Logs the request and response. Raises exception if request fails. Returns the response object. This function simulates the OOBE registration that normally happens when a printer is first powered on.

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of the card header to find

functions.what_it_does: Finds a card body element by locating its header text. Locates elements with class 'card-header' that have exact text matching header_text using :text-is() selector. Asserts that at least one matching header exists. Navigates up one level from the header using '..' to get the card container. Then locates the '.card-body' element within that container. Returns the card body locator. This is used for extracting data from card-based UI layouts in admin interfaces.

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of the card header

functions.what_it_does: Extracts text content from a card body by finding the card header. Calls find_card_body_by_header() to locate the card body element. Calls inner_text() on the card body locator to get all text content. Strips leading and trailing whitespace. Returns the cleaned text string. This is used for extracting field values from card-based admin UI layouts.

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of the card header

functions.what_it_does: Extracts a list of link URLs from a card body by finding the card header. Calls find_card_body_by_header() to locate the card body element. Locates all 'a' (anchor) elements within the card body. Iterates through all link elements using .all(). For each link, extracts the href attribute using get_attribute('href'). Returns a list of all href values. This is used for extracting multiple links from card-based admin UI layouts.

Extracts the last number from the current page URL. Gets the current URL from page.url. Uses regex re.findall(r'\d+', url) to find all sequences of digits in the URL. Returns the last number from the list if any numbers were found, otherwise returns None. This is commonly used to extract IDs from URLs like '/subscriptions/12345' where 12345 is the subscription ID.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text string to extract numbers from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts all numbers (integers and decimals) from a text string. Returns empty list if text is None or empty. Strips whitespace from the text. Uses regex re.findall(r'\d+\.\d+|\d+', text) to find all decimal numbers (e.g., '12.34') and integers (e.g., '56'). Returns the list of matched number strings. Returns empty list if no matches found. This is used for extracting numeric values from UI text that may contain mixed content.

Sends an RTP (Real-Time Printer) device status message to the simulator to trigger supply level updates and impression count reporting. Initializes counter values (bizmodelFlag, lastValidConfigSeqNum, lastReceivedConfigSeqNum, lastValidCredentialSeqNum, supplyInsertionCount, totalImpressionCount, bizlogicImpressionCount, internalReportImpressionCount, snapshotCountersSum). Generates a unique timestamp. Starts with a base64-encoded crsSessionData template. Decodes the template into a mutable bytearray. Uses struct.pack to write the counter values and device_uuid into specific byte offsets in the binary data. Re-encodes the modified data to base64. Defines a decode_crssession function to verify the packed data. Logs the decoded values for debugging. Creates a replacements dictionary with all values including cloud_id, uuid, crsSessionData, counters, and timestamp. Loads the RTP payload template JSON file based on _supplyvariant (RTPDS-PayLoad-IIC.json or RTPDS-PayLoad-IPH.json). Replaces all {{placeholder}} values in the template with actual values. Parses the template as JSON. Recursively coerces numeric string values to integers for specific keys. Logs the final payload. PUTs the payload to the simulator rtpdevicestatustriggers endpoint. Logs and returns the response. This function simulates printer status reporting to trigger supply shipments and subscription state changes.

Generates an offer code (POSA code) from an offer identifier by calling the Agena codes API. Gets an org-aware token using get_org_aware_token(). Constructs the Agena codes URL with the identifier_code. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=1', and Content-Type 'application/json'. POSTs to the codes endpoint. Logs the response. Raises exception if request fails. Extracts the code from response JSON at path ['posaCodes'][0]['code']. Logs and returns the generated code. This code can be used during enrollment to apply the offer.
functions.usage_example: code = offer_request('10232'); # Returns a POSA code like 'ABC123DEF456'

functions.method_signature: def get_offer(region: str, type: str = "free", amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> dict

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for offers with exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount greater than this value in cents

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount less than this value in cents

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an offer from the OFFERS_DATA (loaded from offers.json) based on region and filtering criteria. Validates that region is provided, raising ValueError if not. Validates that region exists in OFFERS_DATA, raising ValueError if not found. Gets the list of offers for the region. Iterates through offers checking if the type matches. If no amount filters are provided, returns the first matching offer. If amount filters are provided, validates that the offer has an amountCents field and converts it to int. Checks the amount against amount_equal (must equal), amount_greater (must be greater than), and amount_less (must be less than) filters. Returns the first offer that matches all criteria. If no matching offer is found, constructs an error message listing the filters that were applied and raises ValueError. This function is used to select appropriate offers for enrollment tests based on test requirements.
functions.usage_example: offer = get_offer('US', type='prepaid', amount_equal=6000); guid = offer['guid']; identifier = offer['identifier']

functions.method_signature: def get_offer_code(offer_type: str = "free", amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> str

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for offers with exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount greater than this value

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount less than this value

functions.what_it_does: Generates an offer code for the current test country based on offer type and amount filters. Gets the country code from GlobalState.country_code. Validates that country_code is set, raising ValueError if not. Calls get_offer() with the region, offer_type, and amount filters to find a matching offer. Extracts the identifier from the offer. Validates that identifier exists, raising ValueError if not. Calls offer_request() with the identifier to generate the POSA code. Logs and returns the generated code. This is a convenience function that combines offer selection and code generation for test setup.
functions.usage_example: code = get_offer_code(offer_type='prepaid', amount_equal=6000); # Returns POSA code for $60 prepaid offer

functions.what_it_does: Captures user information by intercepting the /v3/usermgtsvc/users/me API request on the dashboard page. Logs that it's waiting for the request. Uses page.expect_response() with pattern '**/v3/usermgtsvc/users/me' and 120-second timeout to wait for the request. Reloads the page to trigger the request. When the response is captured, extracts the response object and its request. Attempts to parse the response body as JSON, catching any errors and storing an error message if parsing fails. Constructs a captured_data dictionary containing the request (method, url, headers, body) and response (status, status_text, headers, data). Prints and returns the captured_data. If the request is not captured within the timeout, logs a warning and returns None. This function is used to extract authentication tokens and user data from dashboard API calls.

Retrieves user information by calling the Gemini /api/users/me endpoint using a token captured from the dashboard. Calls user_info_from_dashboard() to capture the API request and extract the authorization token. Removes 'Bearer ' prefix from the token. Constructs the Gemini users/me URL. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept and Content-Type as application/json. GETs the endpoint using the session. Logs the response. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data. This function provides detailed user and subscription information.

Retrieves detailed subscription data from Gemini API for a specific subscription ID. Gets a Gemini client token using get_token_gemini_client(). Constructs the Gemini subscriptions URL with the subscription ID. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept and Content-Type as application/json. GETs the endpoint. Logs the response. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing full subscription details. This function is used for subscription state validation and data extraction.

Retrieves the subscription state for a specific subscription ID. Calls subscription_data_from_gemini() to get full subscription data. Attempts to extract the subscriptionState from the nested JSON path ['userSubInfo']['subscriptionInfo']['minSubInfo']['subscriptionState']. Returns the subscription state string if found. If a KeyError occurs (path doesn't exist), returns None. This function is used for subscription state validation in tests.

Retrieves the subscription state by calling the /api/users/me endpoint via the dashboard. Calls request_me_by_dashboard() to get user data. Attempts to extract the subscriptionState from the nested JSON path ['userMinSubInfo']['minSubInfo'][0]['subscriptionState']. Returns the subscription state string if found. If a KeyError occurs, returns None. This function provides an alternative way to get subscription state using the dashboard API.

Validates that a subscription reaches the expected state within 60 seconds. Records the start time. Enters a polling loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. In each iteration, calls get_subscription_state() to get the current state. If the state matches expected_state, logs success and breaks out of the loop. Otherwise waits 1 second and checks again. After the loop, asserts that the final state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError with a descriptive message if not. This function is used in tests to wait for subscription state transitions after actions like enrollment, suspension, or cancellation.
functions.usage_example: validate_subscription_state('12345', 'ACTIVE'); # Waits up to 60s for subscription to become ACTIVE

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a subscription reaches the expected state within 60 seconds using the dashboard API. Records the start time. Enters a polling loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. In each iteration, calls get_subscription_state_by_me_from_dashboard() to get the current state. If the state matches expected_state, logs success and breaks out of the loop. Otherwise waits 1 second and checks again. After the loop, asserts that the final state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError with a descriptive message if not. This provides an alternative validation method using the dashboard API instead of direct Gemini API calls.
functions.usage_example: validate_subscription_state_by_me(page, 'ACTIVE'); # Waits up to 60s for subscription to become ACTIVE

functions.what_it_does: Removes web services from a virtual printer by calling the simulator API. Logs that it's removing web services for the entity_id. Constructs the simulator webservices URL for the printer. Sends a DELETE request to the endpoint. Logs the response status and text. Checks if status is 200. If not 200, raises RuntimeError. Logs success message. This function simulates disabling web services on a printer, which can be used to test offline scenarios or web services re-enablement flows.

Enables web services on a virtual printer by calling the simulator API. Logs that it's enabling web services for the entity_id. Constructs the simulator webservices URL for the printer. Sends a PUT request to the endpoint. Logs the response status and text. Checks if status is 200. If not 200, raises RuntimeError. Logs success message. This function simulates enabling web services on a printer, which is required for printer connectivity and enrollment.

Returns the list of cartridge colors for the current printer based on its supply variant. Checks the global _supplyvariant variable. If _supplyvariant is 'IPH' (Instant Ink Photo), returns ['CMY', 'K'] indicating a combined color cartridge and separate black cartridge. If _supplyvariant is 'IIC' (Instant Ink Cartridge), returns ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] indicating four separate cartridges for black, cyan, magenta, and yellow. This function is used for supply level validation and cartridge-related test assertions.
functions.usage_example: colors = printer_colors(); # Returns ['CMY', 'K'] for IPH or ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] for IIC

functions.what_it_does: Parses a date string in multiple possible formats and returns a datetime object. Defines a list of date format strings including: '%b %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM), '%Y/%m/%d %I:%M:%S %p' (2025/09/14 02:41:13 PM), '%d %b %Y, %H:%M:%S' (14 Sep 2025, 14:41:13), '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S' (14/09/2025 14:41:13), '%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p' (09/14/2025 2:41:11 PM), '%B %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (September 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM). Iterates through each format attempting to parse the date_string using datetime.strptime(). If parsing succeeds, returns the datetime object. If parsing fails with ValueError, continues to the next format. If all formats fail, raises ValueError with the original date_string. This function handles date parsing for various locale-specific date formats found in the UI.
functions.usage_example: date_obj = parse_flexible_date('Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM'); # Returns datetime object

functions.what_it_does: Extracts a date from text that may contain other content. Cleans the text by replacing multiple whitespace characters with single spaces and stripping leading/trailing whitespace. Defines a list of regex patterns to match various date formats. Iterates through each pattern attempting to find a match using re.search(). If a match is found, extracts the date string from the first capture group. Logs the extracted date string. Calls parse_flexible_date() to parse the date string into a datetime object. Returns the datetime object. If no pattern matches, raises ValueError with the cleaned text. This function is used to extract dates from UI text that may contain labels or other content.
functions.usage_example: date_obj = extract_date_from_text('Next shipment: Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM'); # Returns datetime object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves all available Instant Ink plans for the current country from the Agena API. Constructs the Agena plans URL with country parameter (_tenant_country_short), program parameter 'i_ink', and obsolete parameter 'false'. Gets a Gemini client token. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=3', and Content-Type 'application/json'. GETs the plans endpoint. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing the list of available plans. This function is used to retrieve plan information for plan selection and validation.
functions.usage_example: plans = get_plans_data(); # Returns list of plan dictionaries

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Plan attribute key to filter by

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to match for the specified key

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Calls get_plans_data() to get all plans. Converts the value parameter to int. Filters the plans list to include only plans where the specified key equals the value. If no matching plans are found, raises ValueError with the key and value. Returns the list of matching plans. This function is used to find plans with specific characteristics like page count.
functions.usage_example: plans_100 = get_filtered_plan_data(key='pages', value=100); # Returns all 100-page plans

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves all available yearly Instant Ink plans for the current country from the Agena API. Constructs the Agena plans URL with country parameter (_tenant_country_short), program parameter 'i_yearly_ink', and obsolete parameter 'false'. Gets a Gemini client token. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=3', and Content-Type 'application/json'. GETs the plans endpoint. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing the list of available yearly plans. This function is used to retrieve yearly plan information for annual enrollment testing.
functions.usage_example: yearly_plans = get_yearly_plans_data(); # Returns list of yearly plan dictionaries

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Plan attribute key to filter by

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to match for the specified key

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves yearly plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Calls get_yearly_plans_data() to get all yearly plans. Converts the value parameter to int. Filters the plans list to include only plans where the specified key equals the value. If no matching plans are found, raises ValueError with the key and value. Returns the list of matching plans. This function is used to find yearly plans with specific characteristics like annual page count.
functions.usage_example: yearly_plans_1200 = get_filtered_yearly_plan_data(key='pages', value=1200); # Returns all 1200-page yearly plans

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected billing status code based on the current payment method. Checks if 'credit_card' is in the global _payment_method string. If yes, sets status to 'CPT-100'. If the _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides status to 'STRATUS-PAYMENT-COMPLETE'. Checks if 'direct_debit' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets status to 'DIRECT-DEBIT-SUCCESS'. Checks if 'paypal' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets status to 'PAY-PAL-SUCCESS'. Checks if 'prepaid' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets status to 'PEGASUS-SUCCESS'. Returns the determined status code. This function is used for payment status validation in tests.
functions.usage_example: expected_status = billing_status_code(); # Returns 'CPT-100' for credit card with PGS gateway

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Boolean indicating if this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund event response status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines a nested dictionary mapping payment method types to full and partial refund status codes. Normalizes the _payment_method by extracting the base type (credit_card, direct_debit, paypal, or prepaid_only). Gets the refund status codes for the normalized payment method. If full parameter is True, returns the full refund status code. Otherwise returns the partial refund status code. This function is used for refund event validation in tests.
functions.usage_example: full_refund_status = request_event_response_status(full=True); # Returns 'PAYMENT-GATEWAY-REFUNDED' for credit card

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Boolean indicating if this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines a nested dictionary mapping payment method types to full and partial refund status codes. Normalizes the _payment_method by extracting the base type (credit_card, direct_debit, paypal, or prepaid_only). Gets the refund status codes for the normalized payment method. If full parameter is True, returns the full refund status code. Otherwise returns the partial refund status code. This function is used for refund status validation in tests.
functions.usage_example: full_refund = refund_status(full=True); # Returns 'FULL-REFUND' for credit card

functions.what_it_does: Returns the payment engine name based on the current payment method. Checks if 'credit_card' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'payment_gateway'. If _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides to 'stratus'. Checks if 'direct_debit' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'pgs_direct_debit'. Checks if 'paypal' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'pgs_pay_pal'. Checks if 'prepaid_only' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'pegasus'. Returns the determined payment engine name. This function is used for payment engine validation and routing in tests.
functions.usage_example: engine = payment_engine(); # Returns 'payment_gateway' for credit card with PGS

functions.what_it_does: Returns a list of country names that do not require state/province selection in address forms. Returns a hardcoded list containing: Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Luxembourg, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Singapore, Sweden, Switzerland. This function is used to determine whether state/province field validation should be applied in address entry tests.
functions.usage_example: no_state_countries = countries_without_states_mandate(); if country in no_state_countries: skip_state_validation()

functions.what_it_does: Returns a list of country names that support direct debit payment method. Returns a hardcoded list containing: Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Spain. This function is used to determine whether direct debit payment option should be available in payment method selection tests.
functions.usage_example: direct_debit_countries = countries_with_direct_debit(); if country in direct_debit_countries: test_direct_debit_option()

functions.what_it_does: Checks if an element is actionable (clickable and not obscured by other elements). Gets the bounding box of the element using locator.bounding_box(). Returns False if no bounding box is found. Calculates the center point of the element (center_x, center_y). Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that calls document.elementFromPoint() at the center coordinates. Checks if the element at that point is either the target element itself or contains the target element. Returns True if the element is actionable, False otherwise. Catches any exceptions and returns False. This function is used to verify that elements are truly clickable before attempting interactions.

Parses a JSON string and returns the parsed object. Attempts to parse the json_string using json.loads(). If parsing fails, logs an error with the exception details and re-raises the exception. This is a simple wrapper around json.loads() that adds error logging.

Retrieves printer SKU data from the Agena API for the current country and derivative model. Constructs the Agena printer_skus URL with _tenant_country_short and _derivative_model parameters. Gets a Gemini client token. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=3', and Content-Type 'application/json'. GETs the printer_skus endpoint. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing printer SKU information. This function is used to retrieve printer-specific data for validation and configuration.
functions.usage_example: printer_info = printer_data(); # Returns printer SKU data dictionary

functions.what_it_does: Extracts CSS styling details from a plan card element. Uses element.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that gets the computed styles using window.getComputedStyle(). Extracts and returns a dictionary containing 'border-radius' and 'margin' CSS properties. This function is used for UI styling validation of plan cards.

Extracts comprehensive font and text styling details from an element. Uses element.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that gets the computed styles using window.getComputedStyle(). Extracts and returns a dictionary containing: font-family, font-size, font-weight, font-style, line-height, letter-spacing, text-decoration, text-transform, color, font-variant, text-align, background-color, and fill CSS properties. This function is used for detailed typography and styling validation.
functions.usage_example: font_info = get_font_details(text_element); font_family = font_info['font-family']; font_size = font_info['font-size']

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific CSS property of an element has the expected value. Calls get_font_details() to get all font styling details. Extracts the value of the specified property from the font details dictionary. Asserts that the actual value equals the expected value, raising AssertionError with a descriptive message if not. Returns the actual property value. This function is used for CSS property validation in UI tests.

Normalizes a font-family CSS value by extracting the first font name and removing quotes. Returns the value unchanged if it's empty or None. Splits the value by comma to handle font stacks. Takes the first font name. Removes double quotes and single quotes. Strips whitespace. Returns the normalized font name. This is used for font family comparison where 'Forma DJR UI', sans-serif should match 'Forma DJR UI'.
functions.usage_example: normalized = _normalize_font_family('"Forma DJR UI", sans-serif'); # Returns 'Forma DJR UI'

parameters.name: landing_page
parameters.description: Landing page object with footer element attributes

functions.what_it_does: Validates font styling for all footer elements on the landing page. Defines a list of checks, each containing: attribute name on landing_page object, expected CSS properties dictionary, and label for logging. Iterates through each check. Gets the element from landing_page using getattr(). Asserts the element exists. Calls get_font_details() to get actual font styling. For each expected property, extracts the actual value. If the property is 'font-family', normalizes both actual and expected values using _normalize_font_family(). Asserts actual equals expected, raising AssertionError with descriptive message if not. Logs success for each element. This function performs comprehensive footer typography validation.
functions.usage_example: check_footer_fonts(landing_page); # Validates all footer element fonts

parameters.name: new_account_auth_option
parameters.description: Authentication option for new account: 'use_password' or 'send_sign_in_code'

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account with support for new HPID flow variations. Tests browser connectivity with about:blank. Navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Accepts privacy banner if present. Clicks sign-up button. Clicks create account button. Checks if password screen appears first (sometimes happens). If so, clicks back button to return to email screen. Fills email field with _tenant_email. Clicks next button. Detects if this is a new email flow (firstName field appears) or existing email flow. For new email flow: fills firstName and lastName, validates new_account_auth_option parameter, if 'send_sign_in_code' clicks that button, if 'use_password' clicks use password button and fills password, clicks create button, waits for OTP screen, retrieves and fills verification code, submits OTP, waits for stepper. For existing email flow: clicks use password button, fills password, clicks sign-in button, waits 5 seconds. Navigates to portalshell and waits 60 seconds. Clicks side menu toggle if present. Clicks HP Instant Ink menu item. Returns page object. This is the most comprehensive account creation function supporting multiple HPID flow variations.

Extracts verification code from email HTML with multiple fallback strategies. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse HTML. First attempts to find paragraph element with class 'code' and extract text. If found, returns it. If not found, extracts all text from the HTML using stripped_strings. Defines multiple regex patterns to match verification codes: 'one-time passcode (OTP): 123456', 'one-time passcode...123456', 'The OTP will expire...123456', and generic 6-digit number. Iterates through patterns attempting to find a match. If a match is found, returns the 6-digit code from the first capture group. If no pattern matches, raises RuntimeError. This is a more robust version of extract_verification_code() that handles various email formats.

variables_and_constants.constant_value: requests.Session() with verify=False and proxies configured
Dictionary with gemini_rails_admin_endpoint, agena_cps_and_fulfillment_rails_admin_endpoint, fetch_tenant_endpoint, rails_admin_user, rails_admin_password
Large nested dictionary with configuration for pie, stage, and production stacks including all URLs and client secrets
JSON data loaded from offers.json file

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the largest and most critical helper module in the AURA framework. It contains 79KB of code with 60+ functions covering all major test flow operations. The module is organized into several functional areas: 1) Environment configuration with STACK_CONFIG defining pie/stage/production endpoints, 2) Authentication helpers for HPID, OAuth, Gmail, Stratus, UCDE, and Gemini, 3) Virtual printer creation and management using G2 simulator API, 4) Email verification code retrieval using Gmail API, 5) Payment method data providers, 6) Subscription state validation, 7) Offer code generation, 8) UI interaction utilities, 9) Date parsing and extraction, 10) Font and styling validation. The module uses global variables extensively to maintain state across function calls. The session object is configured with SSL verification disabled and proxy support for HP VPN. The STACK_CONFIG dictionary is the central configuration hub mapping stack names to all environment-specific URLs and secrets. The PrinterData class provides a convenient tuple-based structure for passing printer information. The retry_operation() function provides generic retry logic used throughout the module. The create_virtual_printer() function implements a complex multi-step printer creation process with retry logic at each step. The authentication functions implement OAuth flows and token exchange for various HP services. This module is imported by virtually all test scripts and provides the foundation for test execution.

key_reusable_methods: set_globals — Configures all global framework variables from args dictionary
setup — Initializes framework by calling set_globals with GlobalState values
generate_tenant_email — Generates unique test email with random suffix
authenticate_gmail — Authenticates with Gmail API using refresh token
fetch_verification_code — Retrieves HPID verification code from Gmail with retry
create_hpid — Creates HPID account through signup page
create_ii_v2_account — Creates Instant Ink v2 account with full enrollment flow
create_new_ii_v2_account — Creates account with new HPID flow variations
login_ii_v2_account — Logs into existing Instant Ink v2 account
logout_ii_v2_account — Logs out of Instant Ink v2 account
onboard_hpid_to_ucde — Onboards HPID to UCDE via OAuth flow
get_token_gemini_client — Gets Gemini client access token
get_org_aware_token — Gets organization-aware user token via multi-step exchange
create_virtual_printer — Creates virtual printer via G2 simulator with retry
claim_virtual_printer — Claims virtual printer to tenant via UCDE API
add_address_to_tenant — Adds shipping address via Gemini comfe API
create_and_claim_virtual_printer — Creates and claims printer in one operation
create_and_claim_virtual_printer_and_add_address — Creates printer, claims, and adds address
get_payment_method_data — Retrieves payment test data with dynamic year calculation
send_rtp_devicestatus — Sends RTP device status to trigger supply updates
offer_request — Generates offer code from identifier via Agena API
get_offer — Retrieves offer from offers.json with filtering
get_offer_code — Generates offer code for current country with filters
subscription_data_from_gemini — Gets full subscription data from Gemini API
get_subscription_state — Extracts subscription state from Gemini data
validate_subscription_state — Validates subscription reaches expected state within 60s
get_plans_data — Retrieves all Instant Ink plans from Agena API
get_filtered_plan_data — Filters plans by attribute value
billing_status_code — Returns expected billing status for payment method
payment_engine — Returns payment engine name for payment method
printer_colors — Returns cartridge colors for supply variant
extract_date_from_text — Extracts and parses date from text with multiple formats
is_element_actionable — Checks if element is clickable and not obscured
retry_operation — Generic retry wrapper with configurable attempts and delay
